rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-(2-alkenylphenyl)benzamides with CF3SO2Na to give CF3-containing benzoxazines and oxazolines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (Eox = 0.83 V) to CF3SO2 radical, which fragments to CF3 radical and SO2; cathodic reduction of HFIP generates H2; second anodic oxidation of the cyclized alkyl radical to a cation

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na
Intermediate I: alkyl C-radical from CF3 addition to alkene of N-(2-isopropenylphenyl)benzamide
Intermediate II: benzylic/oxocarbenium cation after radical cyclization onto amide oxygen and anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the alkene of the N-arylbenzamide to give alkyl radical I, which cyclizes onto the amide oxygen; the resulting radical is oxidized at the anode to cation II and deprotonated to deliver the benzoxazine, with cathodic H2 evolution from HFIP.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of TEMPO completely suppressed formation of 2a (0% yield), supporting a radical pathway involving CF3 radical

notes: CV shows CF3SO2Na oxidizes at 0.83 V while substrate 1a oxidizes at 1.32 V, indicating preferential anodic oxidation of the sulfinate rather than the alkene (distinguishing this from alkene radical-cation pathways). Control entry without electricity gave no product, confirming electrochemistry is essential. CF2HSO2Na can replace CF3SO2Na to give the difluoromethyl analogue (2v, 31%).
